column1: Orchestrated comprehensive tax document search across expanded date range

The user wants me to expand the search period from the last 3 months to the entire period from January 1, 2025 to present (February 7, 2026). This is searching for 2025 tax year documents, so I need to search from January 1, 2025 (when the tax year started) to today.
I'll need to run the same searches but with a different date range: after:2025-01-01 instead of after:2025-11-07.
Let me run through the same search categories:
Subject: IMPORTANT TAX RETURN DOCUMENT AVAILABLE

Tax Impact: Form 1099 Stock Plan Consolidated Original and Stock Plan Transactions Supplement for account ending in 9666. Reports income, cost basis, and holding period information for stock transactions. Includes adjusted cost basis and gains/losses needed for your tax return. Likely includes Cohesity equity compensation.
2. Form 1099-INT (Morgan Stanley Private Bank) - Multiple Emails
Tax Impact: Reports interest income from your Morgan Stanley Private Bank accounts. Required for reporting taxable interest on Schedule B.
Subject: ERIC, Your 1098 Tax Form Is Now Available on Our Website
Tax Impact: Reports mortgage interest paid on account ending in 7169. Deductible on Schedule A if you itemize deductions (likely given property values in Atherton).
Subject: Completed: Complete with Docusign: 2025 Keystone Tax Engagement Letter
Tax Impact: Signed engagement letter with your tax preparer for 2025 tax return preparation.
6. Old Mission Ventures LLC - Annual Filing
Sender: Kalicki Collier, PLLC via Adobe Acrobat Sign
Subject: You signed: "2026 Annual List - Old Mission Ventures, LLC"
Tax Impact: LLC annual registration filing. May need to be tracked for K-1 generation or ownership documentation.
7. Bernstein Statements with 2025 Tax Document Information
Subjects: "Bernstein January 2026 Statement" and "Bernstein December 2025 Statement"

Tax Impact: Contains information about availability and timing of 2025 tax documents for your Bernstein brokerage account(s). Watch for 1099-DIV (dividends), 1099-B (sales of securities), and potentially 1099-INT or 1099-MISC.
8. San Mateo County Property Tax Payment
Sender: GovHub (no-reply@grantstreet.com) forwarded by Hyun Ju Park
Subject: County of San Mateo Payment Confirmation

Tax Impact: Property tax payment of $104,136.70 for 162 James Ave, Atherton (Parcel #061-052-020) for 2025 Secured Annual Bill #2025-346085. This represents 1st & 2nd installments. Fully deductible on Schedule A up to the $10,000 SALT cap when combined with state income taxes.
9. Clear Creek Tahoe Community Association - Quarterly Assessments
Clear Creek Tahoe Community Association (notifications@frontsteps.com, CCTCA@calibersoftware.email)
Subjects: Various statements and budget information

Tax Impact: HOA quarterly assessment of $1,600 per quarter ($6,400 annually for 2025). Note: Regular HOA assessments are generally NOT tax deductible. Only special assessments for capital improvements may be added to property basis. Monthly club dues and assessments shown on statements.
Subject: Fw: CFO Equity Grant - confidential

Tax Impact: Contains information about CFO equity grant from Sanjay Poonen. This grant will generate taxable income when RSUs vest or options are exercised. Watch for supplemental W-2 or 1099 reporting of equity compensation income. Important for tracking basis in Cohesity stock.
Subject: Fwd: I have solved the sequencing issue on the sale of 331,000 Tanium shares

Tax Impact: Documentation regarding the sale/donation of 331,000 Tanium shares that is central to the Old Mission Foundation LLC charitable contribution issue. This transaction is the subject of the IRS audit and will be critical for your 2025 tax return if there are any carryforward implications or adjustments from the appeals resolution.
W-2 from Cohesity (typically issued by January 31, but not yet in your email)
Schedule K-1 from Old Mission Foundation LLC and/or Old Mission Ventures LLC (if applicable)
Any additional 1099 forms from other financial institutions
1. IRS Appeals for Old Mission Foundation LLC - $6M in proposed penalties relating to 2021 charitable contribution; ongoing appeals process with rebuttal briefs submitted in February 2026
2. Property Tax Deductions - $104,136.70 San Mateo County property tax (subject to $10,000 SALT cap)
3. Stock Compensation - Cohesity equity grants and E*TRADE stock plan transactions
4. Mortgage Interest - Form 1098 from Morgan Stanley (account 7169)
1. Download all available tax forms from online portals (E*TRADE, Morgan Stanley, Bernstein)
2. Provide all documents to Keystone CPA (Christy Moore, Michael Bosma, Lawrence Balanovsky)
3. Continue monitoring the Old Mission Foundation LLC IRS Appeals process
4. Watch for Cohesity W-2 (should arrive by late January/early February)
5. Confirm whether you'll receive Schedule K-1s from any LLC investments
6. Collect documentation for any other deductible expenses (business expenses, charitable contributions, etc.)
